enableLocalHelp: Direct connection to the inverter via TCP/Protobuf on port 10081. Provides real-time data every few seconds including per-panel power, voltage, current and temperature. Requires the inverter to be on the same network.
devicesHelp: Add your inverters by IP address. Each Hoymiles HMS inverter with integrated WiFi (DTUBI) has its own IP. You can find it in your router's DHCP client list (device name: DTUBI-*).
dataIntervalHelp: How often the inverter is queried for new data (0-300 seconds). Set 0 for fastest possible (~1 second). Lower values give more responsive data but slightly increase network traffic.
slowPollFactorHelp: Configuration and alarm data changes rarely. They are queried only every Nth data cycle to reduce load. Default: 6 (= every 30 seconds at 5s interval).
enableCloudRelay: Cloud relay (forward data to Hoymiles Cloud)
enableCloudRelayHelp: When the adapter connects locally, the inverter can no longer upload data to the Hoymiles Cloud itself. Enable this to let the adapter forward the data on behalf of the inverter, so the cloud portal and app continue to work.
cloudRelayWarning: Note: A local connection occupies the inverter's only communication channel. This prevents it from sending data to the Hoymiles Cloud on its own. Enable the cloud relay so the adapter forwards the data to the cloud on behalf of the inverter. Without the relay, the Hoymiles app and cloud portal will not work correctly.
enableCloudHelp: Connect to the Hoymiles S-Miles cloud to get energy statistics (daily, monthly, yearly), CO2 savings, income calculation and per-inverter power data. All inverters in your account are automatically discovered.
cloudInfo: Uses the same account as the Hoymiles S-Miles app. Your password is stored encrypted. The cloud provides data that is not available locally, such as monthly/yearly energy totals and per-inverter power when not connected locally.
cloudAuthErrorLabel: Last cloud login error (empty = OK; non-empty suspends automatic retries to prevent your Hoymiles account from being locked out)
